title: Meltdown: Reading Kernel Memory from User Space — Lipp et al. (2018)
author: Lipp et al.
description: Out-of-order execution exploit leaking kernel memory directly to user space.
pdf: /assets/reading/Meltdown- Reading Kernel Memory from User Space.pdf

title: Spectre Attacks: Exploiting Speculative Execution — Kocher et al. (2018)
author: Kocher et al.
description: Branch prediction exploitation to leak data across hardware security boundaries.

title: Operating Systems: Three Easy Pieces — Arpaci-Dusseau & Arpaci-Dusseau
author: Arpaci-Dusseau
description: Comprehensive introduction to OS internals — processes, memory, and file systems.

title: Security Engineering — Ross Anderson (3rd ed., Free Online)
author: Ross Anderson
description: Building systems that remain dependable under hostile and adversarial conditions.

title: The Byzantine Generals Problem — Lamport, Shostak & Pease (1982)
author: Lamport et al.
description: Achieving distributed consensus despite unreliable and malicious participants.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications — Martin Kleppmann
author: Martin Kleppmann
description: Deep dive into data systems at scale — storage engines, replication, and reliability.

title: Reflections on Trusting Trust — Ken Thompson (1984)
author: Ken Thompson
description: On trusting trust — the compiler backdoor thought experiment and its implications.

title: Introduction to Algorithms (CLRS) — Cormen, Leiserson, Rivest & Stein
author: Cormen et al.
description: The definitive reference on algorithms and data structures, from sorting to NP-completeness.

title: Smashing the Stack for Fun and Profit — Aleph One (1996)
author: Aleph One
description: Classic guide to stack buffer overflows and shellcode writing on x86 Linux.
image: /assets/reading/Smashing the Stack for Fun and Profit .png

title: Hacking: The Art of Exploitation — Jon Erickson
author: Jon Erickson
description: Hands-on exploitation, shellcode, and low-level hacking fundamentals from scratch.

title: No Silver Bullet: Essence and Accidents of Software Engineering — Fred Brooks (1987)
author: Fred Brooks
description: No single technique yields the dramatic productivity gains software engineers seek.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer — David Thomas & Andrew Hunt
author: Thomas & Hunt
description: Practical wisdom for software craftsmanship, clean code, and long-term career growth.

title: Social Engineering: The Art of Human Hacking — Christopher Hadnagy
author: Christopher Hadnagy
description: The psychology and methodology behind manipulating people as the weakest security link.

title: The Mythical Man-Month — Frederick P. Brooks Jr.
author: Fred Brooks
description: Why adding engineers to a late project makes it later — timeless software project wisdom.

title: Clean Code: A Handbook of Agile Software Craftsmanship — Robert C. Martin
author: Robert C. Martin
description: Principles, patterns, and practices for writing readable, maintainable software.

title: Refactoring: Improving the Design of Existing Code — Martin Fowler
author: Martin Fowler
description: A catalog of refactoring techniques for restructuring code without changing its behavior.

title: The C Programming Language — Brian Kernighan & Dennis Ritchie
author: Kernighan & Ritchie
description: The original C reference — still the clearest introduction to the language and systems thinking.

title: Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs — Abelson & Sussman
author: Abelson & Sussman
description: MIT's classic — abstraction, recursion, interpreters, and the essence of computation.

title: Cracking the Coding Interview — Gayle Laakmann McDowell
author: Gayle L. McDowell
description: 189 programming problems and solutions to prepare for software engineering interviews.

title: Computer Networking: A Top-Down Approach — Kurose & Ross
author: Kurose & Ross
description: Network layers from application down to the physical — the standard networking textbook.

title: The Linux Command Line — William Shotts
author: William Shotts
description: A complete introduction to the Linux command line and shell scripting.

title: MapReduce: Simplified Data Processing on Large Clusters — Dean & Ghemawat (2004)
author: Dean & Ghemawat
description: Google's programming model for processing large datasets across commodity clusters.

title: The Google File System — Ghemawat, Gobioff & Leung (2003)
author: Ghemawat et al.
description: A scalable distributed file system for large data-intensive applications.

title: Modern Operating Systems — Andrew Tanenbaum
author: Andrew Tanenbaum
description: Comprehensive coverage of OS design principles, processes, memory, and file systems.

title: Dynamo: Amazon's Highly Available Key-Value Store — DeCandia et al. (2007)
author: DeCandia et al.
description: Eventual consistency and the techniques behind Amazon's distributed storage system.

title: Head First Design Patterns — Freeman & Robson
author: Freeman & Robson
description: Object-oriented design patterns explained with visual, brain-friendly examples.

title: Attention Is All You Need — Vaswani et al. (2017)
author: Vaswani et al.
description: The transformer architecture that revolutionized NLP and modern deep learning.

title: Deep Work — Cal Newport
author: Cal Newport
description: Rules for focused success in a distracted world — building the ability to concentrate deeply.

title: Raft: In Search of an Understandable Consensus Algorithm — Ongaro & Ousterhout (2014)
author: Ongaro & Ousterhout
description: A consensus algorithm designed to be more understandable than Paxos.

title: The Art of Problem Solving Vol. 1 — Rusczyk & Lehoczky
author: Rusczyk & Lehoczky
description: Competition mathematics fundamentals — the foundation of algorithmic thinking.

title: On the Criteria To Be Used in Decomposing Systems into Modules — Parnas (1972)
author: David Parnas
description: The foundational paper on modularity and information hiding in software design.

title: Compilers: Principles, Techniques & Tools — Aho, Lam, Sethi & Ullman
author: Aho et al.
description: The Dragon Book — comprehensive coverage of compiler design and implementation.
